items.name: SERVIR Agriculture and Food Security: SERVIR is using machine learning techniques to interpret satellite data to improve crop area, crop type, crop condition, and yield estimates at the scale of 10s of meters, aggregated to country, district, province, or country.
items.agency: United States Agency for International Development
items.full_agency: United States Agency for International Development

items.name: SERVIR Water Security: SERVIR is applying deep learning on in situ and global/regional hydrologic forecasts to fill gaps in historic and recent streamflow and river elevation estimates, and improve streamflow forecasts, to a vertical precision of meters to centimeters, potentially for thousands to millions or river reaches worldwide.
items.agency: United States Agency for International Development
items.full_agency: United States Agency for International Development

items.name: SERVIR Ecosystem and Carbon Management: Automation of land cover and change mapping, including forest disturbance due to roads and mining. SERVIR is applying machine learning and deep learning techniques on satellite data to automate the classification and identification of land surface activities at the scale of 10s of meters.
items.agency: United States Agency for International Development
items.full_agency: United States Agency for International Development

items.name: SERVIR Weather & Climate Resilience: Short-term and seasonal weather prediction and hazard mapping. SERVIR is mapping floods and landslides with machine learning classification techniques, on the order of 10m to 10km. SERVIR is exploring the performance of weather foundational models versus numerical weather predictions on the 4-10km scale.
items.agency: United States Agency for International Development
items.full_agency: United States Agency for International Development

items.name: SERVIR Air Quality and Health: Processing modeled air quality forecasts and assimilating large volumes of data from satellite instruments. SERVIR is downscaling satellite- and model-derived air quality predictions with neural networks, down to 5km spatial resolution.

items.full_agency: Administrative Tribunals Support Service of Canada / Service canadien d'appui aux tribunaux administratifs
items.description: Westlaw Edge AI (with AI-Assisted Research) is an AI-augmented legal research platform from Thomson Reuters. It uses a large language model layered on Westlaw’s curated legal content to answer natural-language legal questions, generate summaries with supporting citations, surface jurisdictional law surveys, and provide litigation analytics.

items.name: AI Legal Editing

items.description: Using CanChat to prepare legal summaries.

items.name: AI Proof of Concept

items.description: Identifying tools and developing infrastructure and skills required to implement future AI solutions for ATSSC and the tribunals it supports.

items.name: Milo 2.0
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description: Milo 2.0  is an internal enterprise service management chatbot provided as part of the enterprise service management tool BMC Helix. It will helps users get the technical help they need. It interoperates with the IT ticketing system to streamline IT requests and provides users with answers to frequently asked IT questions. It leverages LLMs and knowledge based articles to enable users to get the help they need in an even more natural and conversational manner. Developed in part by AAFC.

items.name: AgPal Chat
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description: AgPal and generative AI is making it easier for anyone to find information about more than 400 federal, provincial, territorial, and municipal programs and services that support the sustainable growth and competitiveness of the sector. AgPal Chat allows users to find information on the programs and services available to them quicker, giving them easier and better access to funding and support tailored to their needs.

items.name: Annual Crop Inventory Map
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description: Since 2009, the Annual Crop Inventory Map is one of AAFC's most downloaded open datasets and is used for sustainability reporting, policy, and programming by providing metrics of land use and environmental change over time.

items.name: Document Detective
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description: Built upon SSC’s automated tool, AAFC’s Document Detective Tool is a smart system that helps the Access to Information and Privacy (ATIP) team streamline and accelerate the process of finding identical or very similar documents. The tool’s scalable and flexible architecture ensures that it can grow with our needs and can be used in different areas without needing major changes for process optimization and efficiency. Discussions are underway to scale the tool in other departments.

items.name: Milo
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description: Milo is an internal, enterprise IT chatbot first launched in 2019 that helps users get the technical help they need. It interoperates with the IT ticketing system to streamline IT requests and provides users with answers to frequently asked IT questions. At the beginning of 2024, AAFC started implementing improvements to the chatbot, leveraging the power of AI to further streamline processes, enabling users to get the help they need in an even more natural and conversational manner.

items.name: Extraction of Protein Digestibility Data
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description: The project aims to use AI and automation to extract and interpret nutrition data from scientific literature, focusing on amino acid composition. It would then calculate amino acid scores based on various reference patterns and present the results in summary tables or graphs highlighting the limiting amino acid.

items.name: Arthropod Detection and Classification
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description: Computer models are used to find and identify different insect species from images taken from sticky traps. The results are shown in an easy-to-use online dashboard to help track biodiversity.

items.name: Powdery Mildew Detection
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description: The project aims to address the challenge of accurate and efficient identification of plant diseases, specifically focusing on the detection of powdery mildew. Powdery mildew is a common fungal disease that affects a wide range of plant species, leading to decreased crop quality and yield. The project seeks to develop a user-friendly web application embeded with AI functionalities that empowers farmers, gardeners, and agricultural enthusiasts to detect powdery mildew and make informed decisions for disease management.

items.name: ProteBee - AI Analysis Tool for Evaluation
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description:
This project aims to implement a generative AI assistant to support qualitative data analysis. This tool will allow staff to query and summarize content from SharePoint folders using prompts informed by evaluation frameworks. It will generate outputs with precise references, helping evaluators identify insights and refine their analysis.
Operating in a secure, sandboxed environment, the assistant may also reference publicly available AAFC information. By streamlining tasks like summarization and coding, it will free up time for deeper research and stakeholder engagement. The goal is to enhance, not replace, evaluator capacity, enabling more efficient, inclusive, and insightful evaluations.

items.name: Digitizing Insect Labels
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description:
This project aims to automate the transcription of insect collection labels using Generative AI (GenAI) models. The insect collection includes approximately 100,000 specimens with photographed labels that have not yet been transcribed into the central database. 
The solution includes using GenAI models to extract text from these images, both printed and handwritten, and structure the extracted content into a predefined format suitable for direct integration with the existing insect database.

items.name: OneHealth Emergency Tool
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description:
The project is to develop a flexible and scalable system in order to search and compile publicly available One Health risk indicators. Use cases may span zoonotic diseases, antimicrobial resistance, transmissible animal diseases, and plant and animal diseases with significant health and economic impacts. 
The project will aim at collecting and filtering publicly available data sources. Another objective will be to store and export outputs in a structured, editable format. And lastly, developing a cumulative repository while avoiding duplication.

items.name: Data and Digital Requirements Intake Solution
items.full_agency: Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada / Agriculture et agroalimentaire Canada
items.description: The proposed initiative aims to streamline and reduce administrative overhead in the Data and Digital Requirements (DDR) processes by incorporating AI and/or automation technologies.  The processes and tool is used by the Project Management Office as a business intake for new IM/IT business application or solution to automate existing or new business processes, enhance exisiting applications by adding new functionality, upgrade in existing technology enviroment and engage SSC resourcing.

items.name: Automatic detection of guns and gun parts

items.description: Machine learning algorithm in development for postal X-rays to automatically detect guns and gun parts

items.name: Fuzzy Search (SSAName3)
items.full_agency: Canada Border Services Agency / Agence des services frontaliers du Canada
items.description: Fuzzy Search (SSAName3) is a critical software tool used by CBSA to flag high risk travellers and goods. Utilized to conduct fuzzy name searching and matching across a number of CBSA’s lines of business solutions (Travellers, Commercial and Intelligence & Enforcement), particularly mission critical systems. The software processes data across multiple different datasets so they are properly indexed, identified, scored and ranked.

items.name: Client Reporting and Engagement System (CRES)/ReportIn
items.full_agency: Canada Border Services Agency / Agence des services frontaliers du Canada
items.description: The Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA) developed a voluntary Remote Reporting tool to enable individuals subject to reporting conditions are able to report remotely to the CBSA. The app uses automated facial recognition software and geographic data to verify the identity and location of individuals. CBSA staff review every report made by an individual.

items.name: Primary Inspection Kiosk

items.name: Security Screening Automation Project
items.full_agency: Canada Border Services Agency / Agence des services frontaliers du Canada
items.description: The Security Screening Automation (SSA) Project is designed to expedite the review of security screening referrals. While the project seeks to automate many components of the triage function to support officers in their review of security screening referrals, the deployment of a Large Language Model is the only AI component being deployed within the project. Data, provided as part of the referral, is assessed against pre-defined areas of concern. These potential concerns are then identified to screening officers to support their review of the referral. The semantic capabilities of open-source large language models (LLM) are then used to create short narrative summaries to assist the screening officers. The LLM is asked to attempt and identify the existence of certain pre-determined areas of concerns.

items.name: Travellers Compliance Indicators

items.description: Travellers Compliance Indicators (TCI) is a series of algorithms to assist border officers in identifying likely compliant travellers. It is currently in development and will not be in production before 2026.

items.name: BERDI
items.full_agency: Canada Energy Regulator / La Régie de l’énergie du Canada
items.description: BERDI (Biophysical, Socio-Economic, and Regional Data and Information) is an AI-enabled system that improves access to data from Environmental and Socio-economic Assessments (ESAs) submitted to the Canada Energy Regulator since 2003. It enables targeted keyword searches and exploratory browsing of data related to potential impacts of pipeline projects on the environment and on the health and well-being of Canadians. It uses machine learning to extract structured data and metadata from PDF documents and applies safeguards to identify and exclude culturally sensitive Indigenous Knowledge from the dataset. It is available online at www.cer-rec-gc.ca/berdi (English) and www.cer-rec.gc.ca/ciber (French) and through the Open Science Data Portal.

items.full_agency: Canada Revenue Agency / Agence du revenu du Canada

The wait time could be 5-10 days, delaying access and increasing demand on contact centres. In March 2024, the CRA launched the Document Verification Service for taxpayers to gain immediate access by using their smartphone, an accepted government-issued ID, and facial recognition technology.

items.name: CAVIA (Collections and Verification Intelligent Assistant) Phase 2 - ACSES Diary Notes
items.full_agency: Canada Revenue Agency / Agence du revenu du Canada
items.description:
The objective of this project is to implement a self-serve product that enables efficient utilization and search of the diary notes in the ACSES system. A product could make this information more accessible to CVB users.
The solution should have the ability to search across all diary notes to identify specific and relevant information. This includes advanced natural language processing, capturing details that are commonly sought manually, such as previous actions taken on accounts (i.e. Requirement to Pay issued, phone calls made, associated account numbers, legal warnings), and other descriptive actions.
The goal is to improve productivity, accuracy and efficiency for employees who need to access and use the diary notes.

items.name: APIDS (Automated Prohibited Item Detection) Algorithms Deployment
items.full_agency: Canadian Air Transport Security Authority / L’Administration canadienne de sûreté du transport aérien
items.description: Assessment and trial of different APIDS algorithms on multi-view and CT x-rays used at PBS. OEMs and third party companies are developing object recognition algorithms to work in conjunction with explosive detection algorithms to detect potential threats. Materials absorb x-rays at different rates based on their density and atomic number, creating a contrast image of luggage contents.  Explosive detection algorithm analyzes the densities/atomic number and size of items in a bag  and compares the results against a known list of explosives and threat masses prescribed by the regulators to identify items of concern. Algorithms are developed by the equipment suppliers and are updated every several years based on new regulation. Each algorithm is rigorously tested by TSA and ECAC regulators before certification using real explosives. CATSA works with Transport Canada to assess whether new algorithms need to be rolled out to CATSA’s fleet of equipment.

items.name: Darktrace
items.full_agency: Canadian Grain Commission / Commission canadienne des grains
items.description: Darktrace is a cybersecurity system that uses artificial intelligence (AI) to detect, investigate, and respond to cyber threats in real time. AI is used to learn normal patterns of behavior across networks, devices, and users, enabling the system to identify anomalies that may signal malicious activity. The business problem prompting AI use was the growing complexity, speed, and unpredictability of cyberattacks, which traditional rule-based security tools struggled to manage. The objective of the system is to provide autonomous threat detection and response, reducing reliance on manual intervention and minimizing the time to identify and contain threats.

items.name: Drafting Ministerial Correspondence

The goal was to test if Generative AI could draft responses matching or exceeding human-written quality while being more efficient (time, completeness, error rate, etc.). The experiment was integrated into existing workflows with minimal disruption, adhering to Government of Canada policies and privacy standards.Future recommended actions include the implementation and integration of generative AI with existing systems to streamline workflows.

items.name: Grants and Contributions
items.description: Conducted an AI experiment aimed at revolutionizing how grants and contributions (G&Cs) are managed and delivered. Over ten weeks, we explored how Human-Centred AI and machine learning could be leveraged to enhance program discoverability and improve service delivery for clients seeking funding. To this end, we developed an enriched Large Language Model (LLM) based on the Department's program structure and program guidelines. As a result of the experiment, we have identified the potential to scale the approach, including as broadly as across the public sector. The departmental lead for the experiment, as well as the President and Chief Executive Officer of the firm that supported the experiment, will be delivering a live demonstration of the AI-powered discovery tool at the 2025 DPI Professional Development Week to showcase its capabilities in transforming G&Cs service delivery. Originally developed by Systemscope prior to their acquisition by Levio)

items.name: PAT (Personal Assistant Trainer)
items.full_agency: Canadian Space Agency / Agence spatiale canadienne
items.description: PAT is intended to support operators’ space robotics training by creating a state-of-the-art learning experience that includes both theoretical knowledge and practical application. Familiarization with the Mobile Servicing System (MSS, aka Canadarm2) and its robotics work station (RWS) operational environment is the first lesson new recruits receive when taking the robotics training at CSA. Under this initiative, PAT will provide this familiarization training, first for the MSS currently on the International Space Station, and eventually for the Gateway External Robotics System (Canadarm3) located on the new Gateway Station orbiting the Moon. The goal is to provide a comprehensive learning experience for CSA personnel, ensuring they are well equipped to handle current and future space missions. This project aims to set a new benchmark for adaptive learning within the CSA by integrating AI to enhance operational efficiency and effectiveness.

It analyses areas like the Business Number, address of employer, wage being offered, and checking for compliance issues or allegations. The outcome is a concise report that highlights essential details, enabling processing officers to quickly identify key factors and focus on key elements of the applications they are reviewing.
While this summary of information report assists officers, it does not eliminate the need for them to review and consider the various internal sources of information. Officers are required to examine all relevant information independently before making a decision.

items.name: Employment Insurance Machine Learning Workload
items.full_agency: Employment and Social Development Canada / Emploi et Développement social Canada
items.description: Recalculation of Employment Insurance (EI) takes place when circumstances change or new information emerges that could make benefit calculations inaccurate. Where the initial benefit rate or weeks of entitlement were underestimated and EI underpaid, the claim is adjusted to compensate for the financial shortfall. In cases of overpayment, the claim is reduced to recover the excess amount. When no changes are identified and the initial benefit rate and weeks of entitlement were accurate, the claim remains unchanged. The primary objective of the EI Machine Learning Workload is to reduce the time spent by officers on claim reviews by identifying cases where a recalculation will not result in any change. This approach allows officers to focus on more complex reviews that require intervention and precision to ensure clients receive the correct benefit rate and entitlement.

items.name: AI for weather prediction
items.full_agency: Environment and Climate Change Canada / Environnement et Changement climatique Canada
items.description: The Meteorological Services of Canada (MSC) and Science & Technology Branch of ECCC are actively exploring AI for weather prediction. In 2023, MSC and ECCC's Science and Technology Branch published an AI Roadmap that outlines the path for integrating AI into MSC's forecasting processes through to 2030. This roadmap will drive forward key initiatives, such as the exploration of energy-efficient AI weather models (e.g. GraphCast, ForeCastNet and AIFS) and the development of an all-Canadian model called PARADIS (Physically-inspired Advection, Reaction and Diffusion on the Sphere) a hybrid weather prediction system called "spectral nudging" that combines traditional forecasting methods with AI.

items.name: Automated Detection of Unidentified Fish Sounds in Passive Acoustic Data
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description: This completed project used Random Forest and Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) algorithms trained on manually identified fish sounds for detection in passive acoustic recordings. The result is FishSoundFinder, an open-source, user-friendly software tool.

items.name: News topic detection tool
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: Identifying emerging topics that are relevant to DFO's mandate. Manually tracking and assessing the relevance of news content is time-consuming and prone to oversight, especially when trying to align it with existing internal knowledge.

Objective for AI: Use large language models and topic clustering to automatically analyze emerging news topics and determine their relevance to DFO. This approach will also surface what knowledge or resources DFO already possesses to address those topics, enabling faster, more informed responses and strategic planning.

items.name: Oceanographic Anomaly Detection
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description: The goal of this project is to apply machine learning to detect anomalies in conductivity, temperature, depth (CTD) data, highlighting significant physical phenomena in the state of the ocean for closer investigation by scientists.

items.name: IM Chatbot
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description: Chatbot to answer Information Management related questions, based on information available on the IM intranet site.

items.name: GitHub Copilot
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description: GitHub Copilot is being piloted at DFO aimed at enhancing developer productivity and exploring the operational value of AI-assisted coding.

items.name: Remote Observation of Watercraft on Roads
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
The Aquatic Invasive Species Program is proposing to launch a pilot initiative aimed at addressing a critical knowledge gap in our understanding the spread of aquatic invasive species.  Invasive species can attach to boats, trailers, and related equipment—a process commonly referred to as the "stowaway pathway"—which enables their unintentional spread across ecosystems when watercraft are transported between water bodies. The AIS Program is seeking to understand watercraft movement into Canada via international land border crossings. Currently, the extent of this "stowaway" pathway remains largely unquantified.
To address this, the pilot will deploy remote camera systems at select border entry points to capture imagery of incoming vehicles. Leveraging machine learning, the system will automatically identify and flag images containing watercraft.

items.name: PSSIFence
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: DFO inspects hours of underwater camera footage to count and identify fish species through fence counts, which is a structure installed across a river or stream to monitor fish population. The amount of hours needed to review underwater footages will need to increase in order fulfil oblgiations as it is not sustainable.
Objective for AI: Replace manual analysis of fence videos to determine salmon counts by species.  Leveraging advanced computer vision technology, reducing the manual effort required, and providing more accurate and timely data.

items.name: PSSIScaleAging
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: DFO-Sclerochronlogy Lab is responsible for fish age analysis services to clients across British Columbia and the Yukon and is responsible for approximately 80,000 salmon scale aging assessments a year. Technicians require extensive experience and knowledge to provide scale aging assessment. With increasing assessments and limited resources, it will only result in limited analysis performed. 
Objective for AI: Automate and improve the process for lab technicians to analyze scale imprints from salmon to determine its age. By leveraging computer vision, particularly through deep learning models like Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs), the process becomes more efficient and accurate to assess salmon age.

items.name: Pacific RAG Chatbot
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: Information detailing DFO policy and governance, science and research, and main activities in BC and the Yukon for management of fisheries are offered on the DFO Pacific Fisheries internet site. However, it may be difficult to obtain specific key information when searching the website for relevant information. 
Objective for AI:  Aligning on DFO's data strategy and provide information and data that is findable, accessible, interoperable and reuseable is priority, a custom AI chatbot  uses retrievable augmented generation (RAG) to retrieve information from the DFO Pacific Fisheries internet site.

items.name: PSSIOCR
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: Many DFO programs store thousands of documents (scanned, paper-based, unstructured records) that contain valuable information for reporting, tracking, and decision making. Due to the volumn of documents, it is often unfeasable to transfer this information into a digital structured format. This has created significant limitations for the availability, timeliness and quality of data needed to make Pacific salmon management decisions. 
Objective for AI: To help modernize data workflows and ensure long-term preservation and usability of valuable historical data by automating the extraction of document content into a structured format through the use of OCR software.

items.name: Factoid Finder Tool
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: Efficiently extracting and navigating information from machine-readable PDFs. Traditional keyword-based search methods often fall short in identifying contextually relevant content, making it difficult for users to locate specific information within large document libraries.
Objective for AI: The Factoid Finder addresses this issue by extracting text from PDFs to build searchable content libraries. It leverages Small Language Models to implement semantic search techniques, enabling users to retrieve contextually relevant passages from within the documents more effectively.

items.name: Optical Character Recognition Data transcription tool
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: Science observations and research requires transcription and validation from hand-written field notes, including historical information.
Objective for AI: Digitize non-machine-readable (handwritten) documents and extracts characters into a digital format, to deliver productivity gains and improve availability of data from paper forms. Piloted with  ~100 year old salmon observations and present-day salmon stream inspection logs.

items.name: Data Validation Tool
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: Digitizing handwritten or non-machine-readable documents using optical character recognition (OCR) often introduces transcription errors, which can compromise data quality and usability. These inaccuracies make it difficult to rely on the extracted information for analysis or integration into standardized systems.
Objective for AI: Leverage large language models to detect and correct OCR-induced errors by applying intelligent validation rules. The goal is to propose accurate corrections and normalize the extracted data into a consistent, standardized format, ensuring higher reliability and interoperability across systems.

items.name: AI-Assisted CTD Quality Control Tool
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description:
Problem: Quality control of Conductivity, Temperature, Depth (CTD) data is a labor-intensive process that places a significant manual burden on oceanographers. This repetitive task diverts their time and expertise away from more valuable scientific analysis and decision-making activities.
Objective for AI: Implement an AI-powered tool that automatically flags CTD profile scans for deletion or preservation during the quality control process. This will support oceanographers in performing quality control more efficiently and accurately, enhancing productivity and allowing them to focus on tasks that require their specialized knowledge.

items.name: Characterizing Environmental Extremes Using Machine Learning
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description: This project developed a machine learning-based method to identify environmental extremes by segmenting data into regions with consistent conditions (e.g., temperature, oxygen, acidity). Extremes were defined based on historical variability within each region, enabling more targeted and context-aware analysis of environmental changes.

items.name: Improving Streamflow Simulations with Neural Network Post-Processing
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description: This project applied neural networks to post-process streamflow outputs from the WRF-Hydro model, improving alignment with observational data. The approach serves as a downstream calibration step, building synergy between traditional hydrologic calibration methods and modern AI techniques.

items.name: Labels Pilot
items.full_agency: Fisheries and Oceans Canada / Pêches et Océans Canada
items.description: We are looking into using Labels back end for smart labels as well as a free copilot agent for the department to use in order to validate a user's classification.

items.name: Analyse development programming alignment
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: To understand what portion of Canada’s development programming in agriculture and food aligns with the Ceres2030 10 high-impact interventions to meet the United Nation Sustainable Development Goal 2 towards “zero hunger.” The aim of this work is to use machine learning to extract certain project characteristics, features and details at the output and activity level to enhance the details currently found in existing departmental reporting. This will help to elaborate causal relationships of outputs and activities that are buried within project documents and project outcomes presented in reports. This process will lead to building a platform for portfolio analysis and future predictive analysis.  This approach will inform the extent to which interventions and approaches, either individually and jointly, are in alignment with the Ceres2030 Interventions and meeting strategic, programmatic and policy needs and beyond.

items.name: Disinformation dashboard
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Using Watson Natural Language Understanding for named entity recognition to identify key organization, individuals and locations.  IBM Watson® Natural Language Understanding uses deep learning to extract meaning and metadata from unstructured text data.

items.name: Financial risk monitoring
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Leverage AI and ML techniques to to provide regular, proactive risk monitoring on Global Affairs Canada's financial data with an objective to reduce the level of financial risk in mission transactions.  Vendor's off-the-shelf internal audit tool customized with Global Affairs Canada specific AIalgorithm development for financial monitoring.

items.name: Data entry for grants and contributions
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: AI-assisted data entry for Grants and Contributions to improve data accuracy and consistency across several critical data fields. This is expected to lead to better investment planning, prioritizing, accountability, monitoring and reporting of Canada's contribution to international assistance.  Analysis of project documentation and data. Data preparation and identification of most pertinent project documentation. Implementation of various AI natural language processing models to analyse project documentation and data coding. Optimization and fine tuning the model to assess and attain accuracy. AI models to generate project coding and robotic process automation (upcoming) to assist in the automation of data entry. Tools and processes for monitoring and maintenance of the tools.

items.name: Export Control On-Line System (EXCOL)
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: EIPA permits are automatically granted by the NEICS & NEXCOL systems if specific conditions are met, through rules-based techniques (e.g., available quotas for importers/exporters). EXCOL is an interactive and computerized application that allows clients to submit export applications and certificates on-line using Secure Channel.

items.name: Enhanced Reporting Capabilities for International Assistance Grants and Contributions
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Global Affairs Canada is responsible for managing most of Canada's international assistance funding, and its staff are often required to assess alignment between project funding and policy objectives. Assessing projects against evolving and emerging policy markers is time consuming, and GAC looked for risk-informed methods to automate these assessments. Using manual assessments as a gold standard, GAC tested approaches to understand the trade-off between accuracy, transparency, and cost. It implementd methods using generative AI to encode the assessment frameworks and the domain knowledge of experienced evaluators. This approach assigns policy markers based on project summaries generated during GAC’s existing processes. Multiple policy markers have been implemented within a web-based tool which is currently in validation. The tool has an accuracy comparable to expert assessors and produces an accompanying explanation to allow expert oversight.

items.name: Document Analyzer
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Document Analyzer streamlines the evaluation process by automating the structured analysis of multiple documents, enabling faster review and synthesis of findings. It supports standardized assessments, reduces manual effort, and helps evaluators identify trends, risks, and lessons learned more efficiently. The tool also facilitates the adoption of generative AI within evaluation teams, improving both the speed and quality of evaluation outputs for program and project assessments.

items.name: GAC Translate
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Provides the ability for users to upload and get translations of documents up to protected B data, from most world languages. The use case was driven by the need to quickly translate documents with unknown content that might contain personal information, to be able to determine if they require immediate and official translation. Originally built for translation of Protected B Information for the Canadian Ombudsperson for Responsible Enterprise. Since expanded to all GAC users.

items.name: Situational Awareness
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Situational Awareness is a custom application built by the National Research Council, which allows Global Affairs Canada to proactively monitor information originating from traditional news and social media sources.  The platform uses Support Vector Machine libraries to score the news articles as they are ingested.

items.name: Document Cracker
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: To manage the wealth of information, a document search and analysis tool called Document Cracker was developed to help officials quickly find the information they need. Document Cracker enables users to search across a large volume of documents using a sophisticated indexing platform and automatically monitor new documents for specific topics, emerging trends, and mentions of key people, locations, and organizations. These features allow officials to quickly identify pressing issues, formulate a preferred stance, and track the evolving stances of other countries.

items.name: AI-generated briefing notes

items.description: AI-generated briefing notes using internal and external data. (experimentation phase) Experimenting with OpenAI cognitive services for AI-generated briefing notes. This is an attempt to generate a first cut/draft templated briefing for policy analysts on which to review, validate and augment to create a final document. This area is also of interest to Strategic Planning, Policy and Operations who has supported Global Affairs Canada's Data Science team's experimentation.

items.name: SWS-QA-Tool: Use of algorithms, regressions and data science models to assist in data verification processes and analytics for CFO-STATS
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Use of algorithms, regressions and data science models, and automation to assist in data verification processes and analytics.   This AI tool will replace an SAP tool that is no longer supported.  It will assist the CFO's Statistical and Reporting Unit (SWS) to improve it's Back-end QA process, cathing errors more quickly and reliably, leading to better data.  It will also serve  help to evaluate progress on the "Data entry for grants and contributions" project.

items.description: AI Process Assistance Tool is designed to assist the International Assistance Operations. It ingests and analyzes documents related to International Assistance programming, and provides recommended responses to common inquiries. The tool features document ingestion and preprocessing, natural language analysis, and a response recommendation engine. The tool is scalable.

items.name: Copilot Studio
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Supporting a citizen led development, Copilot Studio provides a platform for users to develop their own chatbots and agents, using knowledge bases and data of their choosing. Users can deploy the aganets and chatbots to Sharepoint, MSTeams, and as standalone or integrated apps.

items.name: Enterprise Self Serve AI
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: The 'Chatbots for All' initiative at GAC is designed to enable users across various departments to create and deploy no-code or low-code chatbots. These chatbots will interact with departmental data, files, URLs, SharePoint, and the intranet, thereby improving accessibility and efficiency within the organization. The enterprise AI self-serve platform allows GAC staff to leverage a variety of AI models, tools and capabilities across GAC's enterprise data systems. This platform will also allow staff to prototype workflows and experiment with new technology as it is added to the platform.

items.name: Senior Management Briefs
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: Automates the monitoring and summarization of media coverage. By applying generative AI, the system scans and interprets a wide range of news sources, extracting key developments and producing concise, actionable summaries  for senior management.

items.name: Missions AI-Generated Briefs
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: This tool streamlines the monthly synthesis of Trade-related events and activities reported by Canadian missions across the United States. It transforms these reports into concise, regionally structured summaries that enable staff to spend more time acting on insights rather than preparing them.

items.name: GAC GPT
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: GAC GPT is a secure, internal AI platform developed to emulate AI Chatbot functionality while protecting departmental data. It enables conversational access to curated internal documents, supports domain-specific knowledge, and enhances productivity through Retrieval-Augmented Generation and customizable prompts.

items.name: Financial Accounting Chatbot
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: The solution streamlines access to Global Affairs Canada’s financial accounting policies by deploying an AI-powered chatbot. This tool enables staff to quickly retrieve relevant information, automate routine queries, and improve compliance, efficiency, and autonomy in financial and procurement processes, ultimately reducing time spent searching documents and allowing experts to focus on complex tasks.

items.name: Trade Commissioner Service website chatbot (Digital Experience Platform - DXP)
items.full_agency: Global Affairs Canada / Affaires mondiales Canada
items.description: As a strategic initiative to foster and promote trade, the Trade Commissioner Service (TCS) is deploying a refreshed website to optimize end-user experience for Canadian exporters. In addition to deploying the new website through the Digital Experience Platform project, a public-facing chatbot is targeted for release in the fall 2025 to further enhance the experience. Generative response generation using Microsoft Copilot Studio referencing the TCS website with citation support; semantic matching and trigger phrase refinement for FAQ discovery; keyword-based redirection to Other Government Departments ; and telemetry logging via Azure Application Insights to capture user messages, triggered topics, session activity, and engagement metrics. These capabilities are supported by a multi-environment deployment pipeline (Dev, QA, Staging, Prod), automated unit testing via Pytest, and performance testing using Locust, JMeter, and Azure Load Testing.

items.name: New Export Import Control System (New EICS)

items.description: EIPA permits are automatically granted by the NEICS & NEXCOL systems if specific conditions are met, through rules-based techniques (e.g., available quotas for importers/exporters).

items.name: Conducting literature reviews
items.description: Machine learning to improve efficiency of conducting scientific literature reviews including systematic reviews and scoping reviews.

items.name: Project Cyclops for product label compliance
items.description: Project Cyclops aims to develop an online web application that would enable inspectors to use a digital tool/camera to upload images to assess whether a product label is compliant with Health Canada’s regulations.

items.name:
Science-GPT

Leveraging AI (LLMs, RAG) to assist scientists with pesticide review (grey literature, published literature, scientific studies)
items.description: The volume and complexity of scientific data for chemical assessments is rapidly growing. At PMRA, this has resulted in a growing backlog of chemicals to assess. Scientists at PMRA have identified the potential for Large Language Models (LLMs) to expedite specific review tasks. Building and testing this tool with subject matter experts (SMEs) is key due to the potential for LLMs to generate hallucinations. Science-GPT is a Health Canada Solutions Fund project which seeks to explore, build, test, and validate LLM-enabled AI tools built with scientists to assist scientists with chemical review and build internal capacity on AI. This project is supported by Health Canada Digital Transformation Branch (DTB), Shared Services Canada Science Experimentation Zone (SSC), and The Public Health Agency of Canada (PHAC).

items.name: Food Label AI for Monitoring the food Environment (FLAIME)
items.description: The Food Label AI for Monitoring the food Environment (FLAIME) project aims to collect publicly available data from the internet relating but not limited to food products, nutrition facts tables and list of ingredients. This data is held in a database and machine learning is used to classify, create data models, and generate automated reports.

items.name: Dark Matter
items.description: Dark Matter is a database system developed to store results from predicted machine learning models. At this moment, the database is storing all related information extracted from product monographs, including related data to Drug Product Database and natural health products. This database allows AI systems to infer the importance of document and find relationships between different types of text. It also allows users to fix possible errors in detection done by ML models.

items.name: Observatory
items.description: Observatory is a visualization tool that allows users to find which product monographs are used to treat cancer. Observatory also shows all the Drug Product Database details of any current product and its older versions.

items.name: Grants and Contributions Forecasting
items.full_agency: Housing, Infrastructure and Communities Canada / Logement, infrastructures et collectivités Canada
items.description: Our organization has implemented a new AI forecasting tool to enhance financial processes, focusing on data accuracy, efficiency, and forecasting for our Grants and Contributions.

items.name: Bridge Anomaly Detection Project
items.full_agency: Housing, Infrastructure and Communities Canada / Logement, infrastructures et collectivités Canada
items.description: Use of Machine Learning to detect sensor data anomalies in HICC's major bridges.

items.name: Results Stories LLM Project
items.full_agency: Housing, Infrastructure and Communities Canada / Logement, infrastructures et collectivités Canada
items.description: Use of a fine-tuned LLM to extract numerical data from textual content to improve results reporting.

items.name: Parliamentary Affairs Dashboard
items.full_agency: Housing, Infrastructure and Communities Canada / Logement, infrastructures et collectivités Canada
items.description: Using AI Classification techniques to scrape, identify and present Parliamentary committee meeting topics, questions, interventions and speakers in an easy to consume dashboard.

items.name: Adaptation of Canchat
items.full_agency: Housing, Infrastructure and Communities Canada / Logement, infrastructures et collectivités Canada
items.description: Adaptation of Canchat to HICC environment and departmental needs: particularly knowledge repositories, models and use cases.

items.name: Chatbot to guide potential clients
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: Using a Chatbot deployed on IRCC's Facebook Channel to provide functional guidance to IRCC clients looking to make an application. Leverage dialogue manager, natural language processing to ascertain client intent and provide relevant answers to questions.

items.name: Identify routine applications for faster processing
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: Using advanced analytics to identify routine and straightforward applications for faster processing within these programs: temporary resident visa (visitors), visitor records, and spouse and common-law partner in Canada applications.

items.name: Triaging client enquiries
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: IRCC is using AWS AI services (Textract and Sagemaker) to help triage clients enquiries to improve response time and client experience.

items.name: Automate the review of non-complex applications for Temporary Resident Visas and Work Permits made under the Canada-Ukraine Authorization for Emergency Travel (CUAET)
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project automates the processing of applications for some Temporary Resident Visas and Work Permits made under the Canada-Ukraine Authorization for Emergency Travel initiative (CUAET). The system automates positive eligibility determinations, recording of positive admissibility determinations, and approvals for some applications. The system does not refuse applications or recommend refusing applications, or make negative determinations. All refusals continue to be made by officers. Applications which are removed from the automation process due to the triggering of a rule may still be approved by officers. Automation will also be applied to administrative steps that do not require human intervention. The system never refuses or recommends refusing applications, and does not use artificial intelligence or advanced analytics.

items.name: Passport Automated Decision-Making in IRCC’s Global Case Management System (GCMS)
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project seeks to streamline processing of passport applications. This project seeks to examine how any identified unintended outcomes may be mitigated while supporting passport application processing and particularly how applicants are triaged by the system to streamline identity, eligibility, and entitlement determinations.

items.name: Integrity trends analysis tool
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: An AI-powered solution designed to streamline verification activities by automating the identification and assessment of fraud risks in temporary resident applications. By integrating various data sources and analyzing trends, the tool provides comprehensive risk profiles and detects patterns indicative of fraud. Its intuitive user interface and detailed reporting capabilities enhance the efficiency, accuracy, and productivity of verification activities, enabling proactive measures against potential fraud trends. ITAT uses data elements found and recorded in IRCC’s Global Case  Management System (GCMS) and the Integrated Payment Revenue  Management System (IPRMS). The data consists primarily of information provided directly by clients through their TR applications and supporting  documents. ITAT also uses information that CBSA and other partners have  recorded in the GCMS. IRCC does not use external data sources such as social media.

items.name: Administrative Activities Support Tool
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: AAST is a processing aid to help staff complete administrative tasks for immigration applications, including data matching and bulk email creation. Biometric enrolments are completed by Visa Application Centres (VACs) and IRCC offices. The client is photographed and fingerprinted using Government of Canada software and hardware and basic biographic details are exported and entered into the Biometric Module in AAST. Immigration medical examinations are conducted by Panel Physicians who submit the information electronically to IRCC, which is then entered into the Medical Module in AAST.The Biometric and Medical modules of the AAST use index matching of lists of biographical information between the biometric enrolments and immgration medical examinations, and applications pending those assessments. This helps ensure that biometric enrollments and medical assessments are connected to immigration applications in a timely manner.

items.name: Advanced Analytics Triage of Visitor Record Applications
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project seeks to streamline the eligibility assessment for all visitor record applications to help IRCC decision-makers process applications more efficiently.

items.name: Advanced Analytics Triage of Overseas Temporary Resident Visa Applications
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project seeks to streamline the eligibility assessment for all overseas temporary resident visa applications.

items.name: Family Class Spouses and Partners Overseas Applications
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project seeks to streamline the eligibility assessment for both the Sponsor and Principal Applicant of overseas Spouse or Partner applications.

items.name: International Experience Canada Work Permit Eligibility Model
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project seeks to streamline the eligibility assessment for a subset of IEC WP applications in order to help IRCC decision-makers process applications more efficiently. The IEC WP model triages IEC WP applications by grouping files with similar characteristics based on the legislative, regulatory and contractual requirements for each subprogram and participating country. The model is designed to apply pre-defned triage criteria to triage and identify “light-touch” routine cases where the eligibility portion can receive an automated positive eligibility Assessment. For the Summer 2023 launch, automated eligibility assessments are based on pre-existing business rules for the IEC Working Holiday Program only.

items.name: Spouse or Common-Law Partner in Canada Advanced Analytics

items.description: This project seeks to streamline the eligibility assessment for both the Sponsor and Principal Applicant of Spouse or Common-law Partner in Canada applications.

items.name: Automation Tools to Help Process Privately Sponsored Refugee Applications
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project seeks to streamline the processing of privately sponsored refugee applications using automation technology to help IRCC officers process applications more efficiently.

items.name: Automated Triage and Positive Eligibility Determinations of In-Canada Work Permit Applications
items.full_agency: Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada / Immigration, réfugiés et citoyenneté Canada
items.description: This project seeks to streamline the eligibility assessment for post-graduate work permits (PGWP) and other in-Canada work permit applications in order to help decision makers process applications more efficiently.
