items.description: Leveraging AI tools for research and development (innovation) purposes, in the StatCan protected-B certified AI Exploratory Platform, and the SSC Scientific Experimentation Platform for testing models using unclassified data.

items.name: Machine Learning Models
items.description: Techniques applied to both structured and unstructured data, including machine learning algorithms used for automatic classification, outlier detection, modeling and imputation of missing data, address parsing, deep learning, natural language processing, satellite image processing, and robotic process automation.

items.name: Census Comments Data Capture
items.description: Using Azure AI Document Intelligence service to augment OCR capabilities for capture of paper questionnaires for Census.

items.name: Census Comments Classifier
items.description: Classify Census respondents’ comments into subject matter area or general census themes using natural language processing and deep learning.

items.name: Annual Survey Manufacturing and Logging Industries (ASML) Comments Classification
items.description: A comment classification tool that efficiently extracts more value from respondent comments by triaging comments into groups that are actionable by analysts.The goals were to speed up manual review and actioning of comments through automated triaging, to allow improvements to the EQ experience.

items.name: Annual Survey Manufacturing and Logging Industries (ASML) NAPCS Recommender
items.description: The tool reccomends product codes for given written product descriptions. It is designed to reduce the time analysts spend on coding write-ins with a North American Product Classification System (NAPCS) codes (Human in the loop).

items.name: Capital Expenditure (CapEx) Asset Coding Recommender
items.description: The tool reccomends asset codes for given written asset descritoins. It is designed to reduce the time analysts spend on coding written asset description with an asset code.

items.name: Capital Expenditure (CapEx) AI News Analysis Tool
items.description: Analyse news articles to identify and extract information related on major capital investment projects.

items.name: Census translator
items.description: The census employs specific bilingual terminology and vocabulary, which generic machine translators often fail to translate appropriately. Data scientists fine-tuned a machine translator on past census documents, improving the translation performance and making it a better fit to support the translation of Census material.  It will be used to provide an initial translation of documents, including protected-B and pre-release, which are then manually validated or post-edited by language experts.

items.name: Census Alt-text Generator
items.description: A bilingual, generalized tool that auto-generates alt-text drafts for various images, video captures, charts and maps published by census. Since users will only need to verify the autogenerated text instead of writing from scratch, providing  operational efficiency, quality and consistency.

items.name: StatCan finetuned  LLM based translator
items.description: A customized StatCan machine translator that produces initial translation drafts for language experts and quality check officers to use for their translation requests. It will be integrated into the existing CAT (computer-assisted translator) tools for a streamlined process.  By fine-tuning on top of Azure Custom Translator, the machine translator can accommodate the varying demands of translation requests and accept any Microsoft product (such as Microsoft Word), preserving the input format in its translation output.

items.name: Using LLM for Coding/classification of StatCan statistical programs
items.description: Text to classification LLM coder: exploring horizontal scaling.

items.name: Automatic Capture of the Survey of Household Spending (SHS) Receipts
items.description: While completing the SHS diary, respondents can choose to submit their paper shopping receipts instead of writing down all the daily expenses they’ve made. The approach integrates optical character recognition (OCR) technology with machine learning algorithms to automatically extract and parse information from scanned shopping receipts.

items.name: Census publication standards check (Sphinx)
items.description: LLM-based tool to systematically and automatically check documents for compliance with StatCan standards and flag issues needing human intervension. It helps ensure consistent adherence to standards and helps human reviewers focus on content quality rather than dissemination standards.

items.name: Annual Survey of Manufacturing and Logging (ASML) EQ chatbot
items.description: Implement a generative AI chatbot to answer questions, keep respondents engaged with instant answers,  improve data quality with clear guidance and explanations and reduce burden on data collection clerks, infostats.

items.name: CensAI: Census Reference Materials Chatbot (LLMs)
items.description: Make Census data more accessible by simplifying reference materials.

items.name: Pre-load Air Cargo Targeting (PACT)
items.description: This project seeks to evaluate and mitigate the risk level of cargo inbound to Canada before it is loaded onto an aircraft, adding a layer of security to Canada’s Aviation network. It is used to review cargo manifest for inbound flights in order to identify potential risks to aviation security. https://tc.canada.ca/en/programs/pre-load-air-cargo-targeting-pact. Transport Canada incorporates an artificial intelligence solution into the Pre-load air cargo targeting uses artifical intelligence (specifically supervised machine learning and natural language processing) to interpret text, conduct identity resolution (related to shipper and consignee names and commodity descriptions), and to classify and group free-text data fields found in air cargo information for the purpose of risk assessing inbound shipments to Canada.

items.name: RailState
items.description: Data platform designed to provide real-time and historical information on rail traffic across North America. The platform uses in situ cameras and Al methods to detect, count, and classify train movements to monitor network activity and performance trends, and to enhance situational awareness during disruptions.

items.name: Enhanced Coding for Forecast Model
items.description: Transport Canada used ChatGPT to develop code in "R" programming language to automate data processing and model logic steps used in our freight transportation demand forecasting model. The use of Al saved time in coding the automated processes. The automated processes have freed up time for analysts to work on other things such as the interpretation and communication of model results.

items.name: AI Shadow experiement (policy reaserch)
items.description: In 2024, Transport Canada carried out a structured experiment to test the practical advantages and drawbacks of using AI tools, specifically the Athena platform and ChatGPT, and evaluate the quality and time efficiency of these tools to improve or augment the policy research process (scoping, trends, gathering of info and data, writing/summarizing, etc.) Benefits observed in quicker research capabilities, broader-scanning with more international scope (Athena tool specifically which sources info/data), and potential for upside return on skills investment for analysts in terms of improving policy foresight capacity (again, more specific to Athena tool).

items.name: Travel Policy Chatbot
items.description: The travel policy chatbot is used by TC's corporate finance to answer travel related questions with regards to both TC and NJC travel directive. As the confidence on the model improves this may be opened up to travellers across TC. The chatbot is hosted within TC's Azure cloud instance and has been deployed, release for use is pending completion of functionality for the feedback loop which should be completed prior to the end of the year.

items.name: TC Translate
items.description: Internal translation solution for both free-text and document translation , meant for low risks translations. More reliable English and French translations. Less effort required to ‘fix’ translations. Less expenses in professional translations.

items.name: AI Document Process Automation (ADA)
items.description: The ADA project aims to streamline and automate the processing of paper-based forms received by teams at Transport Canada. By leveraging Power Apps AI Builder technology, ADA is expected to significantly reduce manual data entry efforts, improve processing speed, and enhance data accuracy. This will enable staff to focus on higher-value tasks while minimizing errors and operational inefficiencies.

items.name: Regulatory Platform
items.description: The Regulatory Platform is a web application designed to generate, streamline and structure information on Canadian federal laws and regulations. The Regulatory Platform was created to address a major gap in capabilities for the Government of Canada (GC) regulators and policy analysts to assess, manage, align, and report on the regulatory stock. The platform is a curated data set that allows users to search and filter Acts, Regulations, by department and industry, and obtain comparative metrics on the distribution of regulatory burden in a regulation, department, across government, or by industry sector.

items.name: ATIP Document Inspector
items.description: The Document Inspector will complement ATIP Case Management and redaction tools and transform ATIP analysts’ interactions between ATIP liaisons and document holders. While the journey is completely email based in the current state, the target state will see an AI-powered assistant help ATIP users navigate the journey.

items.name: Risk Evaluation and Conflict Tool (REACT)
items.description: The Risk Evaluation and Conflict Tool (REACT) project is a data-driven initiative led by the Conflict Zone Information Office (CZIO) to enhance Canada’s capability to monitor and assess global conflict-related threats to civil aviation. The tool is designed to streamline the manual and resource-intensive process of open-source media monitoring, data analysis, and risk assessment by leveraging automation and AI technologies.

items.name: Container Volume Forecasting
items.description: Transport Canada has been collaborating with Statistics Canada to develop a machine learning algorithm to forecast container volume arrivals at the Port of Vancouver. The model is not in production/operation.

items.name: Cargo Vessel Forecasting
items.description: In 2024, Transport Canada worked with the Port of Vancouver on a machine learning pilot project to forecast cargo vessel arrivals. The project demonstrated the potential to increase visibility and aid in operational planning.

items.name: Monitoring water levels close to rail lines
items.description: Development of algorithms for assessing water levels/flooding risk with drone, satelite, and hi-rail lidar data.

items.name: Assessing vegetation dryness/fire risk close to rail lines
items.description: Development of algorithms for assessing vegetation dryness with drone lidar data

items.name: Assessing the risks of Transportation of Dangerous Goods operations in Canada
items.description: Development of algorithms to assess large data sets of Transportation of Dangerous Goods incidents and identify leading indicators.

items.name: Monitoring Canadian waterways for obstuctions
items.description: Development of algorithms to detect obstructions within Canadian waterways

items.name: Code generation tools
items.full_agency: Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat / Secrétariat du Conseil du Trésor du Canada
items.description: Piloting AI code-generation tools to increase efficiency in the development and maintenance of software applications. Industry benchmarks have noted significant increases in productivity from software development organizations that leverage this technology. We will be running a pilot and measuring the impacts to team velocity to determine if similar productivity gains are realized in our operating context. Using the Cursor tool developed by Anysphere Inc.

items.name: myGCAssistant (Radia)
items.full_agency: Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat / Secrétariat du Conseil du Trésor du Canada
items.description: TBS needs a safe, bilingual AI platform that helps staff work faster on everyday tasks like finding information, drafting, and answering repeat questions.

items.name: Automation Development to Support Disability Benefit Decision Making
items.full_agency: Veterans Affairs Canada / Anciens Combattants Canada
items.description: The project is to increase VAC use of automation technologies to improve throughput of disability decisions and reduce the overall backlog. One component, Service Health Record Search Tool (SHRST) used an AI handwriting and text extraction function to  read handwritten and typed information within client files as part of extracting Service Health Record information related to disability claims for use in aiding decision making. This benefits the decision making process through saving many hours of manual processing of these files.  Another component uses the same AI technology to to read handwriting within Client Application files as part of automatically transcribing scanned paper applications for disability claims. Developed in part by the VAC  AI Development and Modernization Development teams.

items.name: Claims Summary Tool (CST) - Generative AI for client Information Summarization
items.full_agency: Veterans Affairs Canada / Anciens Combattants Canada
items.description: Experimenting with generative AI to help summarize VAC client documents and information, including personal records and medical assessments.  The goal is to create a complete, concise evidence bundle which can be used to assist adjudicators in disability adjudication.  Currently working with adjudicators to determine if evidence bundle created is comprehensive and accurate. This would improve direct service to Veteran clients through both efficiency and greater accuracy. Developed in part by the VAC AI Development team.

items.name: M365 Copilot Chat
items.full_agency: Veterans Affairs Canada / Anciens Combattants Canada
items.description: Microsoft 365 Copilot Chat is being provided to all VAC employees to offer a secure, AI-powered productivity tool that enhances their ability to summarize content, draft documents, analyze data, and automate tasks.   Not for use with protected or classified information.

items.name: National IT Service Desk AI Assistant
items.full_agency: Veterans Affairs Canada / Anciens Combattants Canada
items.description: Developing generative AI/retrieval-augmented generation system for VAC staff to self service standard IT related issues.

items.name: Sentiment analysis on media articles
items.full_agency: Veterans Affairs Canada / Anciens Combattants Canada
items.description: Meltwater is a product that monitors and provides analytics for media articles.

items.name: Code generation tools (Pilot)
items.full_agency: Veterans Affairs Canada / Anciens Combattants Canada
items.description: Piloting AI code-generation tools to increase efficiency in the development and maintenance of software applications. This pilot represents a low risk use of generative AI technology in support of internal software development. Industry benchmarks have noted significant increases in productivity from software development organizations that leverage this technology. We will be running a pilot and measuring the impacts to team velocity to determine if similar productivity gains are realized in our operating context.

items.name: Determining documents of business value
items.full_agency: Veterans Affairs Canada / Anciens Combattants Canada
items.description: IBM Watson Explorer was used as part of a proof of concept to try to automatically determine whether electronic documents were of business value for retention purposes.

items.name: Sentiment analysis of comments
items.full_agency: Veterans Affairs Canada / Anciens Combattants Canada
items.description: Hootsuite is the product that is used to manage all of Veterans Affairs Canada's social media platforms and provides analytics on the performance

items.name: Form type identification
items.full_agency: Veterans Affairs Canada / Anciens Combattants Canada
items.description: AI is being used to help identify form types being submitted and received through a VAC-developed healthcare provider portal. This results in minimizing manual administrative processing when receiving documents from healthcare providers.

items.name: Topic classification of comments
items.full_agency: Veterans Affairs Canada / Anciens Combattants Canada
items.description: This script is a tool that Veterans Affairs Canada uses to categorize topics of interest of Veterans in order to be able to better understand the issues that are most important to them.
